steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to explain what 'plosive' means using the notes. The answer must define the term itself, not describe a single phoneme like /p/ in isolation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The phoneme /p/ in the word pan is plosive and bilabial.’: Frames /p/'s labial articulation as the main point, making “plosive” a parenthetical descriptor of one phoneme rather than the concept being explained — the term is not defined. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The phoneme /p/ in the word pan is plosive and is produced using both lips.’: Labels /p/ as plosive and describes its articulation but never defines what plosive means — gives a specific instance rather than explaining the category. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The linguistic term fricative is used for units of sound in which air flow is partially blocked.’: Defines fricative, not plosive — wrong term entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — defines plosive as a sound in which airflow is at first fully blocked: Directly defines the term using the key feature from the notes — complete airflow stoppage followed by release. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
